items.situation: two friends recommending a comedy podcast and a cooking series to each other

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You've got to listen to that improv comedy podcast I mentioned. The hosts make up a whole town in every episode. I cannot stop laughing on the bus.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that sounds fun. Is it long? My commute's only twenty minutes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The episodes are short, like fifteen minutes each. Perfect for a quick ride.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Nice, I'll try it. Honestly, if you want something to watch instead, that street-food cooking series is incredible. The night-market episode made me so hungry.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? I keep seeing it pop up. Is it relaxing or kind of intense?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's super calm, no shouting chefs. Just start with episode one, the dumpling stall. You'll be hooked.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly doing?

options.A: Recommending a podcast and a series to each other
options.B: Planning to host a night-market dinner party together
options.C: Complaining about the length of their daily commutes
options.D: Deciding which cooking class to enroll in next term

questions.explanation: 对话中两人互相推荐一档即兴喜剧播客和一部街头美食剧，A 概括最准确。B、C、D 均凭空发挥或只抓住对话中的次要细节（通勤、夜市），不是主旨。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do with the cooking series?

options.A: Skip it because the chefs are too loud for him
options.B: Wait until the woman finishes watching it first
options.C: Suggest the woman begin with the dumpling stall episode
options.D: Watch every episode before his commute tomorrow

questions.explanation: 男生已看过该剧并明确建议女生从'第一集，饺子摊'开始，故 C 对。A 与他说'no shouting chefs'相反；B、D 都没有对话依据，且 D 过度推断。

items.situation: a student returning a too-small sweater at a store counter

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to return this sweater. I ordered a medium online but it's way too small in the arms.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No problem. Do you want a refund, or an exchange for a larger size?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: An exchange would be great, if you have it in a large.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check the back. Hmm, we're out of large in this shop, but the branch across the river has two in stock.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's a bit far for today. Can you just refund it for now?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Of course. I'll put it back on your card. It usually shows up in three business days.

questions.stem: Why is the woman returning the sweater?

options.A: The color was different from the website photo
options.B: It is too small in the arms for her
options.C: It arrived with a tear near the collar
options.D: She accidentally ordered two of the same item

questions.explanation: 女生明确说'too small in the arms'，故 B 对。A、C、D 都未在对话中提及，属凭空发挥。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Travel to the branch across the river today
options.B: Wait at the counter for a large to arrive
options.C: Choose a different color in the same size
options.D: Get a refund back on her payment card

questions.explanation: 女生说过河取货太远，让店员先退款，店员答应退到卡上，故 D 对。A 与她说'a bit far for today'相反；B、C 没有对话依据。

items.situation: two students arranging where and when to study together before a statistics exam

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We really need to study together for the stats exam. The chapter on regression is killing me, and you seemed to get it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, I'd be up for that. I was thinking the library, but the group rooms are always booked by Wednesday.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. What about the empty seminar room in the math building? It's quiet and there's a whiteboard for working problems.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's actually a better idea. Whiteboard practice is exactly what I need for regression. Tomorrow at seven?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Seven works. I'll bring my problem sets so we can work through the ones I keep getting wrong.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Great, and I'll print the formula sheet the professor posted. See you there.

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: Why the library group rooms are always fully booked
options.B: How regression differs from other statistics topics
options.C: Setting up a study session before a statistics exam
options.D: Choosing a major that requires less math coursework

questions.explanation: 对话核心是两人商定考前一起复习统计的时间和地点，C 正确。A 只是对话中提到的一个细节（图书馆订满）；B、D 偏离主题，凭空发挥。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely bring to the session?

options.A: Her problem sets with the questions she got wrong
options.B: The formula sheet the professor posted online
options.C: A reservation slip for the library group room
options.D: A new whiteboard to set up in the seminar room

questions.explanation: 女生明确说会带上自己做错的习题集，故 A 对。B 是男生负责打印的公式表，属张冠李戴；C 与他们改用研讨室相反；D 过度推断（研讨室本就有白板）。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a train schedule for a day trip to a coastal town

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So for our day trip Saturday, what time does the train to Harborville leave?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: There's one at eight and another at ten. The eight gets us there before the shops open, which is kind of pointless.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: True. Let's take the ten then. How long is the ride?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just over an hour. And the last train back is at six, so we won't have to rush.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Six? That gives us a full afternoon. Should we buy tickets online tonight?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, let's book them now. Weekend trains to the coast fill up fast.

questions.stem: Which train do the speakers decide to take?

options.A: The eight o'clock train to Harborville
options.B: The last train, which leaves at six
options.C: A train that takes just under an hour
options.D: The ten o'clock train to Harborville

questions.explanation: 女生说'Let's take the ten'，男生同意，故 D 对。A 被他们否决（去太早商店没开）；B 是返程车而非去程；C 与'just over an hour'矛盾。

questions.stem: What will the speakers most likely do tonight?

options.A: Walk to the station to check the timetable
options.B: Buy their train tickets online in advance
options.C: Cancel the trip because trains are full
options.D: Take the eight o'clock train the next morning

questions.explanation: 两人决定今晚就在网上订票以防周末车票售罄，故 B 对。A、C 没有依据；D 与他们选十点车矛盾且时间错乱。

items.situation: two students talking about a new restaurant downtown and which dish to try

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you been to that new noodle place downtown? Everyone in my dorm keeps raving about it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I went last week. The spicy beef noodle soup is the thing to get. It's what they're known for.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Spicy? I can't really handle a lot of heat. Is there a milder option that's still good?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. The sesame cold noodles are mild and honestly just as popular. Get those instead.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, those sound perfect. Is it expensive though? I'm on a tight budget this month.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not at all, most bowls are under ten dollars. Go around two to skip the lunch crowd.

questions.stem: What dish is the restaurant best known for?

options.C: A budget lunch set under ten dollars

questions.explanation: 男生说招牌是 spicy beef noodle soup（'what they're known for'），故 A 对。B 是他另外推荐的清淡替代品，不是招牌；C 是价格信息；D 未提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely order?

options.A: The spicy beef noodle soup despite the heat
options.B: Nothing, because the place is too expensive
options.C: The sesame cold noodles, which are milder
options.D: A second bowl to share with her dormmates

questions.explanation: 女生不能吃辣，男生推荐清淡的芝麻凉面，女生回应'those sound perfect'，故 C 对。A 与她不吃辣相反；B 与男生说价格便宜相反；D 凭空发挥。
